source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A cube is coloured on the outside as if it was made up of four white and four black cubes where no cubes of the same colour are next to each other (see picture). Which of the following figures represents a possible net of the coloured cube?
<image1>
<image2>
extraction_notes: options field contains only option labels; option figure content appears to be image-based
answer is only the option label because no richer option text is provided in the record

image_dependency: task asks which figure is a possible net of the coloured cube, so the candidate figures must be inspected visually
asset registry shows an image exists and registry_passed=true
multi_step: solver must apply the colouring constraint ('no cubes of the same colour are next to each other') to the cube faces
solver must compare that constraint against multiple net candidates and reason about how each net folds into a cube
this is spatial + combinational reasoning rather than direct recognition
there are 5 answer choices, requiring selection among alternatives
verifiability: answer_type is option/choice with a single provided answer 'E'
a proposed solution can be checked by folding the net and verifying face adjacencies/colour pattern
asset registry indicates question and answer are present and registry_passed=true

decision_reasons: asset registry is complete: question, answer, and primary image are all present
registry passed with no recorded issues, so core semantic assets are available
initial scoring is strong across image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability
although the problem is strongly image-dependent and requires spatial net folding, the needed image asset exists, so this is not a blocking risk
